---
title: "[SOLVED] Frostwire not connecting"
date: 2008-01-03
forum: Absolute Beginner Talk
---

### Post by unknown user on 2008-01-03
[B][COLOR="Green"]I installed Frostwire last night and I was hoping to use it.  However, all it kept on saying was Connecting to a network, when I tried to do a search for something.
Is there anything that I need to do to get it to connect to the servers, or should it find and connect to them automatically, like WinMX & Limewire does in windows?[/COLOR][/B]

---

### Post by billgoldberg on 2008-01-03
If you have connection problems do this:

1. Go to the terminal

  ```
  sudo nautilus
```

2. Browse to gnutella.net

 /home/[USERNAME]/.frostwire/gnutella.net

( press ctrl+h in the home folder to see the hidden files)

3. Open gnutella.net (or create it if it doesn&#8217;t exist)

4. Delete everything inside the file

5. Replace it with

```
http://mc3.electronicbox.net/gnutella.net
```

6. Save file and restart Frostwire.


This is a common bug with frostwire. It's not only a problem on linux, but also on windows.

---

### Post by unknown user on 2008-01-04
Hi there,
Thanks for the help, all is working now.  I left it a day and tried it again yesterday and all is fine.  Wow like the selection on there.

---

### Post by tarreaga on 2008-02-24
Another Option.

I have a 2Wire Router.  Found a fix online.  Did not have to forward a port or change any Frostwire settings.  Go to this site [http://www.mybloop.com/FTA/fixconnecting.zip](http://www.mybloop.com/FTA/fixconnecting.zip).  Download and install than restart Frostwire.  Worked for me.

---
